revision.comment: 新しいページ：榊山古墳（岡山県岡山市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 榊山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tsukuriyama 1st Sakakiyama Kofun.JPG|280px]] |画像キャプション = 墳丘 |別名 = 造山第1古墳 |所属 = 造山古墳群 |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]新庄下 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 18.87 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 48|経度秒 = 6.87 |ISO = JP-33 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径35m |埋葬施設 = 割竹形木棺 |出土品 = 神獣鏡・馬形帯鉤・龍文透金具・多孔鈴・砥石 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「造山古墳 第一、二、三、四、五、六古墳」のうち第一古墳 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Okayama |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 榊山古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|34|40|18|N|133|48|7|E}} | zoom = 15 | float = right | width = 250| height =300 | caption = {{center|造山古墳群分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|40|25.63|N|133|48|13.35|E}} | label1=[[造山古墳]] | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-南南西.svg | mark-size1=110 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|40|18.87|N|133|48|6.87|E}} | label2='''榊山古墳
（第1古墳）''' | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|40|21.23|N|133|48|4.35|E}} | label3=第2古墳 | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=方墳.svg | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|40|18.05|N|133|48|1.00|E}} | label4=第3古墳 | label-pos4=left | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.svg | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|40|16.37|N|133|48|2.55|E}} | label5=第4古墳 | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=帆立貝形古墳-西.svg | mark-size5=30 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|34|40|12.75|N|133|48|6.25|E}} | label6=[[千足古墳|千足古墳
（第5古墳）]] | label-pos6=right | label-color6=black | mark6=帆立貝形古墳-北北西.svg | mark-size6=30 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|34|40|9.85|N|133|48|5.15|E}} | label7=第6古墳 | label-pos7=bottom | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.svg | mark-size7=30 }} '''榊山古墳'''（さかきやまこふん、造山第1古墳）は、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]新庄下にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。造山古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「造山古墳 第一、二、三、四、五、六古墳」のうち第一古墳）。 == 概要 == [[ファイル:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|参考：出土地不明 馬形帯鉤}}{{small|伝榊山古墳出土の馬形帯鉤（宮内庁蔵）に似る。岡山県立博物館蔵、岡山シティミュージアム企画展示時に撮影。}}]] 岡山県南部、[[足守川]]西岸の丘陵上に築造された古墳である。[[造山古墳]]の前方部前面に位置し、周辺の古墳5基とともに造山古墳の[[陪塚]]群を形成しており（造山古墳と陪塚6基で造山古墳群と総称）、他の陪塚よりも造山古墳に最も近く最も高い位置にある。[[1912年]]（[[明治]]45年）に[[千足古墳]]（第5古墳）とともに乱掘されているが、発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は円形とみられ、直径35メートル程度を測る{{Sfn|西田和浩|2020}}。墳丘外表では[[須恵器]]・陶質土器が出土している{{Sfn|榊山古墳（古墳）|1989}}。埋葬施設は墳頂部における[[コウヤマキ]]製の割竹形木棺で、乱掘のため現在も墳頂部中央が陥没している{{Sfn|西田和浩|2020}}。木棺からの副葬品は、千足古墳の出土品と混乱した可能性はあるが、馬形帯鉤6のほか神獣鏡・龍文透金具・多孔鈴・砥石がある{{Sfn|榊山古墳（古墳）|1989}}{{Sfn|西田和浩|2020}}。特に、馬形帯鉤は朝鮮半島南部で製作された可能性がある遺物、龍文透金具・多孔鈴は中国東北部の前燕で製作された可能性がある遺物として注目される{{Sfn|土屋隆史}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]前半頃と推定される{{Sfn|西田和浩|2020}}。造山古墳の築造後、他の陪塚群とともに築造されたと推測される。陪塚群のうち千足古墳が九州地方とのつながりを示すのに対して、榊山古墳は朝鮮半島とのつながりを示しており、吉備の王（造山古墳被葬者）が畿内[[ヤマト王権]]を介さず各地と独自のネットワークを築いていた様子が示唆される{{Sfn|西田和浩|2020}}。 古墳域は[[1921年]]（[[大正]]10年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「造山古墳 第一、二、三、四、五、六古墳」のうち第一古墳）。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1912年]]（[[明治]]45年）、千足古墳・榊山古墳の乱掘（出土品は宮内省帝室博物館に移送）。 * [[1913年]]（[[大正]]2年）、和田千吉が現地調査（[[1919年]]に報告）。 * [[1921年]]（大正10年）3月3日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「造山古墳 第一、二、三、四、五、六古墳」のうち第一古墳）。 == 関連施設 == * 岡山市造山古墳ビジターセンター（岡山市北区新庄下） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=大谷猛|year=1989|chapter=榊山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|榊山古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Wikicite|reference=土屋隆史 {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.okayama.jp/kurashi/cmsfiles/contents/0000005/5424/000354583.pdf 「造山古墳群と東北アジア -榊山古墳出土品を中心に-」]}}|ref={{Harvid|土屋隆史}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=西田和浩|year=2020|chapter=|title=吉備の超巨大古墳 造山古墳群|series=シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」148|publisher=新泉社|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|西田和浩|2020}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=和田千吉|title=備中國都窪郡新庄下古墳|date=1919-07-05|journal=考古學雜誌|publisher=考古學會|volume=9|number=11|pages=663-675|url=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=島崎東|title=備中榊山古墳採集の遺物について|date=1982-03-20|journal=岡山県史研究|publisher=岡山県|volume=|number=3|pages=96-104|url=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=岡山市教育委員会文化財課・岡山市埋蔵文化財センター|author=|year=2015|chapter=|title=千足古墳 -第1-第4次発掘調査報告書-|publisher=岡山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=岡山市埋蔵文化財センター|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=超巨大古墳の時代 吉備の至宝・千足古墳、榊山古墳出土品の里帰り展|series=岡山市埋蔵文化財センター特別展 平成28年度|publisher=岡山市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[造山古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Sakakiyama Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2258|造山古墳 第一、二、三、四、五、六古墳}} {{デフォルトソート:さかきやまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:岡山市北区の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:岡山県にある国指定の史跡]]

{{Japanese burial mound |Name = Sakakiyama Kofun |Image = [[File:Tsukuriyama 1st Sakakiyama Kofun.JPG|280px]] |Caption = Tumulus |Alternate name = Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster |Location = Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|18.87|N|133|48|6.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sakakiyama Kofun}} |Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} |Size = Diameter 35m |Burial facility = Split-bamboo wooden coffin |Artifacts = {{ill|Divine beast mirror|ja|神獣鏡}}, horse-shaped belt hook, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bell, whetstone |Mound type = |Era = Early 5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6") |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Okayama |Icon = 円墳 |Label = Sakakiyama Kofun |Label position = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|34|40|18|N|133|48|7|E}} | zoom = 15 | float = right | width = 250| height =300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|40|25.63|N|133|48|13.35|E}} | label1={{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-南南西.svg | mark-size1=110 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|40|18.87|N|133|48|6.87|E}} | label2='''Sakakiyama Kofun
(No. 1)''' | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|40|21.23|N|133|48|4.35|E}} | label3=No. 2 Kofun | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=方墳.svg | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|40|18.05|N|133|48|1.00|E}} | label4=No. 3 Kofun | label-pos4=left | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.svg | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|40|16.37|N|133|48|2.55|E}} | label5=No. 4 Kofun | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=帆立貝形古墳-西.svg | mark-size5=30 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|34|40|12.75|N|133|48|6.25|E}} | label6={{ill|Senzoku Kofun|ja|千足古墳}}
(No. 5 Kofun) | label-pos6=right | label-color6=black | mark6=帆立貝形古墳-北北西.svg | mark-size6=30 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|34|40|9.85|N|133|48|5.15|E}} | label7=No. 6 Kofun | label-pos7=bottom | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.svg | mark-size7=30 }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in shape and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6"). == Overview == [[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated. The mound appears circular, measuring approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} made of {{ill|koya-maki|ja|コウヤマキ}}. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection. The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster. == History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site. == Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama Kofun Visitor Center (Okayama City, Kita Ward, Shinjo-shimo) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takeo Otani|year=1989|chapter=Sakakiyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Sakakiyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Wikicite|reference=Takashi Tsuchiya {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.okayama.jp/kurashi/cmsfiles/contents/0000005/5424/000354583.pdf "The Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster and Northeast Asia -Focusing on Artifacts from Sakakiyama Kofun-"]}}|ref={{Harvid|Takashi Tsuchiya}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kazuhiro Nishida|year=2020|chapter=|title=The Supergiant Kofun of Kibi: The Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|series=Learn from Archaeological Sites Series, Vol. 148|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kazuhiro Nishida|2020}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article:}} * {{Cite journal|author=Chikichi Wada|title=Kofun in Shinjo-shimo, Kibi Province|date=1919-07-05|journal=Journal of Archaeology|publisher=Archaeological Society|volume=9|number=11|pages=663-675}} * {{Cite journal|author=Azuma Shimazaki|title=Artifacts Recovered from Sakakiyama Kofun, Bitchū|date=1982-03-20|journal=Okayama Prefecture Historical Studies|volume=|number=3|pages=96-104}} * {{Cite book|editor=Okayama City Education Board Cultural Heritage Section|author=|year=2015|chapter=|title=Senzoku Kofun: Reports on Excavation No. 1 to No. 4|publisher=Okayama City Education Board}} * {{Cite book|editor=Okayama City Archaeological Research Center|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=The Era of the Supergiant Kofun: Returning Treasures from Senzoku Kofun and Sakakiyama Kofun|series=Okayama City Archaeological Research Center Special Exhibition|publisher=Okayama City Education Board}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakakiyama Kofun}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2258|Tsukuriyama Kofun (No. 1 to No. 6)}} [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kita Ward, Okayama]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[ja:榊山古墳]] [[ja:榊山古墳]]

Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster. == History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site. == Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama (No. 5 Kofun) | label-pos6=right | label-color6=black | mark6=帆立貝形古墳-北北西.svg | mark-size6=30 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|34|40|9.85|N|133|48|5.15|E}} | label7=No. 6 Kofun | label-pos7=bottom | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.svg | mark-size7=30 }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in shape and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6"). == Overview == [[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated. The mound appears circular, measuring approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} made of {{ill|koya-maki|ja|コウヤマキ}}. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection. The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster. == History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site. == Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama (No. 1)''' | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|40|21.23|N|133|48|4.35|E}} | label3=No. 2 Kofun | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=方墳.svg | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|40|18.05|N|133|48|1.00|E}} | label4=No. 3 Kofun | label-pos4=left | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.svg | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|40|16.37|N|133|48|2.55|E}} | label5=No. 4 Kofun | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=帆立貝形古墳-西.svg | mark-size5=30 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|34|40|12.75|N|133|48|6.25|E}} | label6={{ill|Senzoku Kofun|ja|千足古墳}}
(No. 5 Kofun) | label-pos6=right | label-color6=black | mark6=帆立貝形古墳-北北西.svg | mark-size6=30 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|34|40|9.85|N|133|48|5.15|E}} | label7=No. 6 Kofun | label-pos7=bottom | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.svg | mark-size7=30 }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in shape and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6"). == Overview == [[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated. The mound appears circular, measuring approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=koya-maki|ja|コウヤマキ}}. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection. The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster. == History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site. == Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama (No. 1)''' | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|40|21.23|N|133|48|4.35|E}} | label3=No. 2 Kofun | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=方墳.svg | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|40|18.05|N|133|48|1.00|E}} | label4=No. 3 Kofun | label-pos4=left | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.svg | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|40|16.37|N|133|48|2.55|E}} | label5=No. 4 Kofun | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=帆立貝形古墳-西.svg | mark-size5=30 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|34|40|12.75|N|133|48|6.25|E}} | label6={{ill|Senzoku Kofun|ja|千足古墳}}
(No. 5 Kofun) | label-pos6=right | label-color6=black | mark6=帆立貝形古墳-北北西.svg | mark-size6=30 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|34|40|9.85|N|133|48|5.15|E}} | label7=No. 6 Kofun | label-pos7=bottom | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.svg | mark-size7=30 }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in shape and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6"). == Overview == [[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated. The mound appears circular, measuring approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=koya-maki|ja|コウヤマキ}}. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection. The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster. == History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site. == Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama Kofun Visitor Center (Okayama City, Kita Ward, Shinjo-shimo) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takeo Tsuchiya {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.okayama.jp/kurashi/cmsfiles/contents/0000005/5424/000354583.pdf "The Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster and Northeast Asia -Focusing on Artifacts from Sakakiyama Kofun-"]}}|ref={{Harvid|Takashi Tsuchiya}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kazuhiro Nishida|year=2020|chapter=|title=The Supergiant Kofun of Kibi: The Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|series=Learn from Archaeological Sites Series, Vol. 148|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kazuhiro Nishida|2020}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article:}} * {{Cite journal|author=Chikichi Wada|title=Kofun in Shinjo-shimo, Kibi Province|date=1919-07-05|journal=Journal of Archaeology|publisher=Archaeological Society|volume=9|number=11|pages=663-675}} * {{Cite (No. 1)''' | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|40|21.23|N|133|48|4.35|E}} | label3=No. 2 Kofun | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=方墳.svg | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|40|18.05|N|133|48|1.00|E}} | label4=No. 3 Kofun | label-pos4=left | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.svg | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|40|16.37|N|133|48|2.55|E}} | label5=No. 4 Kofun | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=帆立貝形古墳-西.svg | mark-size5=30 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|34|40|12.75|N|133|48|6.25|E}} | label6={{ill|Senzoku Kofun|ja|千足古墳}}
(No. 5 Kofun) | label-pos6=right | label-color6=black | mark6=帆立貝形古墳-北北西.svg | mark-size6=30 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|34|40|9.85|N|133|48|5.15|E}} | label7=No. 6 Kofun | label-pos7=bottom | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.svg | mark-size7=30 }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in shape and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6"). == Overview == [[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated. The mound appears circular, measuring approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=koya-maki|ja|コウヤマキ}}. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection. The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster. == History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site. == Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama Kofun Visitor Center (Okayama City, Kita Ward, Shinjo-shimo) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takeo Otani|year=1989|chapter=Sakakiyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Sakakiyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Wikicite|reference=Takashi Tsuchiya {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.okayama.jp/kurashi/cmsfiles/contents/0000005/5424/000354583.pdf "The Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster and Northeast Asia -Focusing on Artifacts from Sakakiyama Kofun-"]}}|ref={{Harvid|Takashi Tsuchiya}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kazuhiro Nishida|year=2020|chapter=|title=The Supergiant Kofun of Kibi: The Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|series=Learn from Archaeological Sites Series, Vol. 148|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kazuhiro Nishida|2020}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article:}} * {{Cite journal|author=Chikichi Wada|title=Kofun in Shinjo-shimo, Kibi Province|date=1919-07-05|journal=Journal of Archaeology|publisher=Archaeological Society|volume=9|number=11|pages=663-675}} * {{Cite While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster. == History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site. == Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama (No. 1)''' | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|40|21.23|N|133|48|4.35|E}} | label3=No. 2 Kofun | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=方墳.svg | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|40|18.05|N|133|48|1.00|E}} | label4=No. 3 Kofun | label-pos4=left | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.svg | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|40|16.37|N|133|48|2.55|E}} | label5=No. 4 Kofun | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=帆立貝形古墳-西.svg | mark-size5=30 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|34|40|12.75|N|133|48|6.25|E}} | label6={{ill|Senzoku Kofun|ja|千足古墳}}
(No. 5 Kofun) | label-pos6=right | label-color6=black | mark6=帆立貝形古墳-北北西.svg | mark-size6=30 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|34|40|9.85|N|133|48|5.15|E}} | label7=No. 6 Kofun | label-pos7=bottom | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.svg | mark-size7=30 }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in shape and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6"). == Overview == [[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated. The mound appears circular, measuring approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=koya-maki|ja|コウヤマキ}}. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection. The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster. == History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site. == Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama Kofun-"]}}|ref={{Harvid|Takashi Tsuchiya}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kazuhiro Nishida|year=2020|chapter=|title=The Supergiant Kofun of Kibi: The Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|series=Learn from {{Infobox ancient site |Name = Sakakiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukuriyama 1st Sakakiyama Kofun.JPG|280px]] |Caption = Tumulus |Alternate name = Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster |location = Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|18.87|N|133|48|6.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sakakiyama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} |Size = Diameter 35m |Burial facility = Split-bamboo wooden coffin |Artifacts = {{ill|Divine beast mirror|ja|神獣鏡}}, horse-shaped belt hook, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bell, whetstone |Mound type = |Era = Early 5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6") |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Okayama |Icon = 円墳 |Label = Sakakiyama Kofun |Label position = top }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|40|18|N|133|48|7|E}} | zoom = 15 | float = right | width = 250| height =300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|40|25.63|N|133|48|13.35|E}} | label1={{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-南南西.svg | mark-size1=110 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|40|18.87|N|133|48|6.87|E}} | label2='''Sakakiyama Kofun
(No. 1)''' | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|40|21.23|N|133|48|4.35|E}} | label3=No. 2 Kofun | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=方墳.svg | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|40|18.05|N|133|48|1.00|E}} | label4=No. 3 Kofun | label-pos4=left | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.svg | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|40|16.37|N|133|48|2.55|E}} | label5=No. 4 Kofun | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=帆立貝形古墳-西.svg | mark-size5=30 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|34|40|12.75|N|133|48|6.25|E}} | label6={{ill|Senzoku Kofun|ja|千足古墳}}
(No. 5 Kofun) | label-pos6=right | label-color6=black | mark6=帆立貝形古墳-北北西.svg | mark-size6=30 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|34|40|9.85|N|133|48|5.15|E}} | label7=No. 6 Kofun | label-pos7=bottom | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.svg | mark-size7=30 }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in shape and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6"). == Overview == [[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated. The mound appears circular, measuring approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=koya-maki|ja|コウヤマキ}}. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection. The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster. == History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site. == Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama Kofun Visitor Center (Okayama City, Kita Ward, Shinjo-shimo) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takeo Otani|year=1989|chapter=Sakakiyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Sakakiyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Wikicite|reference=Takashi Tsuchiya {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.okayama.jp/kurashi/cmsfiles/contents/0000005/5424/000354583.pdf "The Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster and Northeast Asia -Focusing on Artifacts from Sakakiyama Kofun-"]}}|ref={{Harvid|Takashi Tsuchiya}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kazuhiro Nishida|year=2020|chapter=|title=The Supergiant Kofun of Kibi: The Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|series=Learn from Archaeological Sites Series, Vol. 148|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kazuhiro Nishida|2020}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article:}} * {{Cite journal|author=Chikichi Wada|title=Kofun in Shinjo-shimo, Kibi Province|date=1919-07-05|journal=Journal of Archaeology|publisher=Archaeological Society|volume=9|number=11|pages=663-675}} * {{Cite journal|author=Azuma Shimazaki|title=Artifacts Recovered from Sakakiyama Kofun, Bitchū|date=1982-03-20|journal=Okayama Prefecture Historical Studies|volume=|number=3|pages=96-104}} * {{Cite book|editor=Okayama City Education Board Cultural Heritage Section|author=|year=2015|chapter=|title=Senzoku Kofun: Reports on Excavation No. 1 to No. 4|publisher=Okayama City Education Board}} * {{Cite book|editor=Okayama City Archaeological Research Center|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=The Era of the Supergiant Kofun: Returning Treasures from Senzoku Kofun and Sakakiyama Kofun|series=Okayama City Archaeological Research Center Special Exhibition|publisher=Okayama City Education Board}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} == External Links == (No. 1)''' | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|40|21.23|N|133|48|4.35|E}} | label3=No. 2 Kofun | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=方墳.svg | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|40|18.05|N|133|48|1.00|E}} | label4=No. 3 Kofun | label-pos4=left | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.svg | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|40|16.37|N|133|48|2.55|E}} | label5=No. 4 Kofun | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=帆立貝形古墳-西.svg | mark-size5=30 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|34|40|12.75|N|133|48|6.25|E}} | label6={{ill|Senzoku Kofun|ja|千足古墳}}
(No. 5 Kofun) | label-pos6=right | label-color6=black | mark6=帆立貝形古墳-北北西.svg | mark-size6=30 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|34|40|9.85|N|133|48|5.15|E}} | label7=No. 6 Kofun | label-pos7=bottom | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.svg | mark-size7=30 }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in shape and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6"). == Overview == [[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated. The mound appears circular, measuring approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=koya-maki|ja|コウヤマキ}}. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection. The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster. == History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site. == Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama Kofun Visitor Center (Okayama City, Kita Ward, Shinjo-shimo) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takeo Otani|year=1989|chapter=Sakakiyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Sakakiyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Wikicite|reference=Takashi Tsuchiya {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.okayama.jp/kurashi/cmsfiles/contents/0000005/5424/000354583.pdf "The Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster and Northeast Asia -Focusing on Artifacts from Sakakiyama Kofun-"]}}|ref={{Harvid|Takashi Tsuchiya}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kazuhiro Nishida|year=2020|chapter=|title=The Supergiant Kofun of Kibi: The Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|series=Learn from Archaeological Sites Series, Vol. 148|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kazuhiro Nishida|2020}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article:}} * {{Cite (No. 5 Kofun) | label-pos6=right | label-color6=black | mark6=帆立貝形古墳-北北西.svg | mark-size6=30 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|34|40|9.85|N|133|48|5.15|E}} | label7=No. 6 Kofun | label-pos7=bottom | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.svg | mark-size7=30 }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in shape and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6"). == Overview == [[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated. The mound appears circular, measuring approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=koya-maki|ja|コウヤマキ}}. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection. The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster. == History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site. == Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster. == History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site. == Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama Kofun Visitor Center (Okayama City, Kita Ward, Shinjo-shimo) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takeo (No. 1)''' | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|40|21.23|N|133|48|4.35|E}} | label3=No. 2 Kofun | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=方墳.svg | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|40|18.05|N|133|48|1.00|E}} | label4=No. 3 Kofun | label-pos4=left | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.svg | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|40|16.37|N|133|48|2.55|E}} | label5=No. 4 Kofun | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=帆立貝形古墳-西.svg | mark-size5=30 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|34|40|12.75|N|133|48|6.25|E}} | label6={{ill|Senzoku Kofun|ja|千足古墳}}
(No. 5 Kofun) | label-pos6=right | label-color6=black | mark6=帆立貝形古墳-北北西.svg | mark-size6=30 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|34|40|9.85|N|133|48|5.15|E}} | label7=No. 6 Kofun | label-pos7=bottom | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.svg | mark-size7=30 }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in shape and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6"). == Overview == [[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}} Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated. The mound appears circular, measuring approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=koya-maki|ja|コウヤマキ}}. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection. The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster. == History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site. == Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama Kofun Visitor Center (Okayama City, Kita Ward, Shinjo-shimo) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takeo It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6"). == Overview == [[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated. The mound appears circular, measuring approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=koya-maki|ja|コウヤマキ}}. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection. The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster. == History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site. == Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama Kofun Visitor Center (Okayama City, Kita Ward, Shinjo-shimo) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takeo (No. 1)''' | label-pos2=right | label-color2=black | mark2=円墳.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|40|21.23|N|133|48|4.35|E}} | label3=No. 2 Kofun | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=方墳.svg | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|40|18.05|N|133|48|1.00|E}} | label4=No. 3 Kofun | label-pos4=left | label-color4=black | mark4=円墳.svg | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|40|16.37|N|133|48|2.55|E}} | label5=No. 4 Kofun | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=帆立貝形古墳-西.svg | mark-size5=30 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|34|40|12.75|N|133|48|6.25|E}} | label6={{ill|Senzoku Kofun|ja|千足古墳}}
(No. 5 Kofun) | label-pos6=right | label-color6=black | mark6=帆立貝形古墳-北北西.svg | mark-size6=30 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|34|40|9.85|N|133|48|5.15|E}} | label7=No. 6 Kofun | label-pos7=bottom | label-color7=black | mark7=円墳.svg | mark-size7=30 }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in shape and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6"). == Overview == [[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}} Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated. The mound appears circular, measuring approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺}} made of {{ill|Sciadopitys verticillata|lt=koya-maki|ja|コウヤマキ}}. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection. The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster. == History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site. == Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama Kofun Visitor Center (Okayama City, Kita Ward, Shinjo-shimo) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takeo Otani|year=1989|chapter=Sakakiyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Sakakiyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Wikicite|reference=Takashi Tsuchiya {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.okayama.jp/kurashi/cmsfiles/contents/0000005/5424/000354583.pdf "The Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster and Northeast Asia -Focusing on Artifacts from Sakakiyama Kofun-"]}}|ref={{Harvid|Takashi Tsuchiya}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kazuhiro Nishida|year=2020|chapter=|title=The Supergiant Kofun of Kibi: The Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|series=Learn from Archaeological Sites Series, Vol. 148|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kazuhiro Nishida|2020}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article:}} * {{Cite journal|author=Chikichi Wada|title=Kofun in Shinjo-shimo, Kibi Province|date=1919-07-05|journal=Journal of Archaeology|publisher=Archaeological Society|volume=9|number=11|pages=663-675}} * {{Cite Kofun|series=Okayama City Archaeological Research Center Special Exhibition|publisher=Okayama City Education Board}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun|ja|造山古墳}} == External Links == The tumulus is round in shape and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6").
== Overview ==
[[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated.
The mound appears circular, measuring approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|kōyamaki]]. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection.
The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}.
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster.
== History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Sakakiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukuriyama 1st Sakakiyama Kofun.JPG|280px]] |Caption = Tumulus |Alternate name = Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster |location = Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|18.87|N|133|48|6.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sakakiyama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Diameter 35m |Burial facility = Split-bamboo wooden coffin |Artifacts = {{ill|Divine beast mirror|ja|神獣鏡}}, horse-shaped belt hook, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bell, whetstone |Mound type = |Era = Early 5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6") |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Okayama |Icon = 円墳 |Label = Sakakiyama Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in shape and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6").
== Overview ==
[[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated.
The mound aqqears circular, measuring aqqroximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|kōyamaki]]. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection.
The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}.
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster.
== History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site.

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Sakakiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukuriyama 1st Sakakiyama Kofun.JPG|280px]] |Caption = Tumulus |Alternate name = Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster |location = Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|18.87|N|133|48|6.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sakakiyama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Diameter 35m |Burial facility = Split-bamboo wooden coffin |Artifacts = {{ill|Divine beast mirror|ja|神獣鏡}}, horse-shaped belt hook, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bell, whetstone |Mound type = |Era = Early 5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6") |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Okayama |Icon = 円墳 |Label = Sakakiyama Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in shape and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6").
== Overview ==
[[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated.
The mound aqqears circular, measuring aqqroximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|kōyamaki]]. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection.
The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}.
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster.
== History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site.
== Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama Kofun Visitor Center (Okayama City, Kita Ward, Shinjo-shimo)

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Sakakiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukuriyama 1st Sakakiyama Kofun.JPG|280px]] |Caption = Tumulus |Alternate name = Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster |location = Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|18.87|N|133|48|6.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sakakiyama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Diameter 35m |Burial facility = Split-bamboo wooden coffin |Artifacts = {{ill|Divine beast mirror|ja|神獣鏡}}, horse-shaped belt hook, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bell, whetstone |Mound type = |Era = Early 5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6") |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Okayama |Icon = 円墳 |Label = Sakakiyama Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in shape and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6").
== Overview ==
[[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated.
The mound aqqears circular, measuring aqqroximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|kōyamaki]]. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection.
The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}.
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster.
== History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site.
== Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama Kofun Visitor Center (Okayama City, Kita Ward, Shinjo-shimo)

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Sakakiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukuriyama 1st Sakakiyama Kofun.JPG|280px]] |Caption = Kofun |Alternate name = Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster |location = Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|18.87|N|133|48|6.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sakakiyama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Diameter 35m |Burial facility = Split-bamboo wooden coffin |Artifacts = {{ill|Divine beast mirror|ja|神獣鏡}}, horse-shaped belt hook, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bell, whetstone |Mound type = |Era = Early 5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6") |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Okayama |Icon = 円墳 |Label = Sakakiyama Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in shape and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6").
== Overview ==
[[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated.
The mound aqqears circular, measuring aqqroximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|kōyamaki]]. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection.
The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}.
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster.
== History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site.
== Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama Kofun Visitor Center (Okayama City, Kita Ward, Shinjo-shimo)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takeo Otani|year=1989|chapter=Sakakiyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Sakakiyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Wikicite|reference=Takashi Tsuchiya {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.okayama.jp/kurashi/cmsfiles/contents/0000005/5424/000354583.pdf "The Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster and Northeast Asia -Focusing on Artifacts from Sakakiyama Kofun-"]}}|ref={{Harvid|Takashi Tsuchiya}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kazuhiro Nishida|year=2020|chapter=|title=The Supergiant Kofun of Kibi: The Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|series=Learn from Archaeological Sites Series, Vol. 148|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kazuhiro Nishida|2020}}}}
== Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article:}} * {{Cite journal|author=Chikichi Wada|title=Kofun in Shinjo-shimo, Kibi Province|date=1919-07-05|journal=Journal of Archaeology|publisher=Archaeological Society|volume=9|number=11|pages=663-675}} * {{Cite journal|author=Azuma Shimazaki|title=Artifacts Recovered from Sakakiyama Kofun, Bitchū|date=1982-03-20|journal=Okayama Prefecture Historical Studies|volume=|number=3|pages=96-104}} * {{Cite book|editor=Okayama City Education Board Cultural Heritage Section|author=|year=2015|chapter=|title=Senzoku Kofun: Reports on Excavation No. 1 to No. 4|publisher=Okayama City Education Board}} * {{Cite book|editor=Okayama City Archaeological Research Center|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=The Era of the Supergiant Kofun: Returning Treasures from Senzoku Kofun and Sakakiyama Kofun|series=Okayama City Archaeological Research Center Special Exhibition|publisher=Okayama City Education Board}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakakiyama Kofun}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2258|Tsukuriyama Kofun (No. 1 to No. 6)}}

The tumulus is round in shape and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}.
