Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated.
The mound appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s circular, measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|kōyamaki]]. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection.
The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}.
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster.
== History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site.

Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated.
The mound appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s circular, measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|kōyamaki]]. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection.
The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}.
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster.
== History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site.

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Sakakiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukuriyama 1st Sakakiyama Kofun.JPG|280px]] |Caption = Kofun |Alternate name = Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster |location = Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|18.87|N|133|48|6.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sakakiyama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |Size = Diameter 35m |Burial facility = Split-bamboo wooden coffin |burial goods = {{ill|Divine beast mirror|ja|神獣鏡}}, horse-shaped belt hook, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bell, whetstone |Mound type = |Era = Early 5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6") |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |Map = Japan Okayama |Icon = 円墳 |Label = Sakakiyama Kofun |Label position = top }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in shape and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6").
== Overview ==
[[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated.
The mound appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s circular, measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|kōyamaki]]. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection.
The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}.
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster.
== History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site.

revision.comment: doing more mass replacements to fix the issues
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Sakakiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukuriyama 1st Sakakiyama Kofun.JPG|280px]] |Caption = Kofun |Alternate name = Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster |location = Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|18.87|N|133|48|6.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sakakiyama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Diameter 35m |Burial facility = Split-bamboo wooden coffin |burial goods = {{ill|Divine beast mirror|ja|神獣鏡}}, horse-shaped belt hook, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bell, whetstone |Mound type = |Era = Early 5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6") |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |map = Japan Okayama |Icon = 円墳 |map_dot_label = Sakakiyama Kofun |map_dot_label position = top }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in shape and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6").
== Overview ==
[[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated.
The mound appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s circular, measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|kōyamaki]]. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection.
The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}.
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster.
== History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site.
== Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama Kofun Visitor Center (Okayama City, Kita Ward, Shinjo-shimo)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takeo Otani|year=1989|chapter=Sakakiyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Sakakiyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Wikicite|reference=Takashi Tsuchiya {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.okayama.jp/kurashi/cmsfiles/contents/0000005/5424/000354583.pdf "The Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster and Northeast Asia -Focusing on Artifacts from Sakakiyama Kofun-"]}}|ref={{Harvid|Takashi Tsuchiya}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kazuhiro Nishida|year=2020|chapter=|title=The Supergiant Kofun of Kibi: The Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|series=Learn from Archaeological Sites Series, Vol. 148|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kazuhiro Nishida|2020}}}}
== Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article:}} * {{Cite journal|author=Chikichi Wada|title=Kofun in Shinjo-shimo, Kibi Province|date=1919-07-05|journal=Journal of Archaeology|publisher=Archaeological Society|volume=9|number=pp|pages=663-675}} * {{Cite journal|author=Azuma Shimazaki|title=Artifacts Recovered from Sakakiyama Kofun, Bitchū|date=1982-03-20|journal=Okayama Prefecture Historical Studies|volume=|number=3|pages=96-104}} * {{Cite book|editor=Okayama City Education Board Cultural Heritage Section|author=|year=2015|chapter=|title=Senzoku Kofun: Reports on Excavation No. 1 to No. 4|publisher=Okayama City Education Board}} * {{Cite book|editor=Okayama City Archaeological Research Center|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=The Era of the Supergiant Kofun: Returning Treasures from Senzoku Kofun and Sakakiyama Kofun|series=Okayama City Archaeological Research Center Special Exhibition|publisher=Okayama City Education Board}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakakiyama Kofun}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2258|Tsukuriyama Kofun (No. 1 to No. 6)}}

{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Sakakiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukuriyama 1st Sakakiyama Kofun.JPG|280px]] |Caption = Kofun |Alternate name = Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster |location = Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|18.87|N|133|48|6.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sakakiyama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Diameter 35m |Burial Facility = Split-bamboo wooden coffin |burial goods = {{ill|Divine beast mirror|ja|神獣鏡}}, horse-shaped belt hook, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bell, whetstone |Mound type = |Era = Early 5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6") |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |map = Japan Okayama |Icon = 円墳 |map_dot_label = Sakakiyama Kofun |map_dot_label position = top }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in shape and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6").
== Overview ==
[[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated.
The mound appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s circular, measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|kōyamaki]]. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection.
The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}.
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster.
== History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site.
== Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama Kofun Visitor Center (Okayama City, Kita Ward, Shinjo-shimo)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takeo Otani|year=1989|chapter=Sakakiyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Sakakiyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Wikicite|reference=Takashi Tsuchiya {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.okayama.jp/kurashi/cmsfiles/contents/0000005/5424/000354583.pdf "The Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster and Northeast Asia -Focusing on Artifacts from Sakakiyama Kofun-"]}}|ref={{Harvid|Takashi Tsuchiya}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kazuhiro Nishida|year=2020|chapter=|title=The Supergiant Kofun of Kibi: The Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|series=Learn from Archaeological Sites Series, Vol. 148|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kazuhiro Nishida|2020}}}}
== Related Reading == {{small|Not directly cited in the article:}} * {{Cite journal|author=Chikichi Wada|title=Kofun in Shinjo-shimo, Kibi Province|date=1919-07-05|journal=Journal of Archaeology|publisher=Archaeological Society|volume=9|number=pp|pages=663-675}} * {{Cite journal|author=Azuma Shimazaki|title=Artifacts Recovered from Sakakiyama Kofun, Bitchū|date=1982-03-20|journal=Okayama Prefecture Historical Studies|volume=|number=3|pages=96-104}} * {{Cite book|editor=Okayama City Education Board Cultural Heritage Section|author=|year=2015|chapter=|title=Senzoku Kofun: Reports on Excavation No. 1 to No. 4|publisher=Okayama City Education Board}} * {{Cite book|editor=Okayama City Archaeological Research Center|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=The Era of the Supergiant Kofun: Returning Treasures from Senzoku Kofun and Sakakiyama Kofun|series=Okayama City Archaeological Research Center Special Exhibition|publisher=Okayama City Education Board}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakakiyama Kofun}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|2258|Tsukuriyama Kofun (No. 1 to No. 6)}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Sakakiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukuriyama 1st Sakakiyama Kofun.JPG|280px]] |Caption = Kofun |alternate_name = Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster |location = Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|18.87|N|133|48|6.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sakakiyama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Diameter 35m |Burial Facility = Split-bamboo wooden coffin |burial goods = {{ill|Divine beast mirror|ja|神獣鏡}}, horse-shaped belt hook, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bell, whetstone |Mound type = |Era = Early 5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6") |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |map = Japan Okayama |Icon = 円墳 |map_dot_label = Sakakiyama Kofun |map_dot_label position = top }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in shape and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6").
== Overview ==
[[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated.
The mound appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s circular, measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|kōyamaki]]. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection.
The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}.
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster.
== History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site.

The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|kōyamaki]]. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection.
The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}.
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster.
== History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site.

The tumulus is round in shape and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. == Overview ==
[[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated.
The mound appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s circular, measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|kōyamaki]]. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection.
The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}.
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster.
== History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site.

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Sakakiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukuriyama 1st Sakakiyama Kofun.JPG|280px]] |Caption = Kofun |alternate_name = Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster |location = Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|18.87|N|133|48|6.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sakakiyama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Diameter 35m |Burial Facility = Split-bamboo wooden coffin |burial goods = {{ill|Divine beast mirror|ja|神獣鏡}}, horse-shaped belt hook, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bell, whetstone |Mound type = |Era = Early 5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6") |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |map = Japan Okayama |Icon = 円墳 |map_dot_label = Sakakiyama Kofun |map_dot_label position = top }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in type and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6").
== Overview ==
[[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated.
The mound appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s circular, measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|kōyamaki]]. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection.
The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}.
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster.
== History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site.

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Sakakiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukuriyama 1st Sakakiyama Kofun.JPG|280px]] |Caption = Kofun |alternate_name = Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster |location = Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|18.87|N|133|48|6.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sakakiyama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Diameter 35m |burial = Split-bamboo wooden coffin |burial goods = {{ill|Divine beast mirror|ja|神獣鏡}}, horse-shaped belt hook, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bell, whetstone |Mound type = |Era = Early 5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6") |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |map = Japan Okayama |Icon = 円墳 |map_dot_label = Sakakiyama Kofun |map_dot_label position = top }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in type and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6").
== Overview ==
[[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated.
The mound appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s circular, measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|kōyamaki]]. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection.
The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}.
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster.
== History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site.
== Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama Kofun Visitor Center (Okayama City, Kita Ward, Shinjo-shimo)
== References == {{Reflist}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Sakakiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukuriyama 1st Sakakiyama Kofun.JPG|280px]] |Caption = Kofun |alternate_name = Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster |location = Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|18.87|N|133|48|6.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sakakiyama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Diameter 35m |burial = Split-bamboo wooden coffin |burial goods = {{ill|Divine beast mirror|ja|神獣鏡}}, horse-shaped belt hook, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bell, whetstone |Mound type = |Era = Early 5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |Historic site = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6") |Tangible cultural property = |Special notes = |map = Japan Okayama |Icon = 円墳 |map_dot_label = Sakakiyama Kofun |map_dot_label position = top }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in type and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6").
== Overview ==
[[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated.
The mound appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s circular, measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|kōyamaki]]. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection.
The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}.
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster.
== History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site.

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Sakakiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukuriyama 1st Sakakiyama Kofun.JPG|280px]] |Caption = Kofun |alternate_name = Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster |location = Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|18.87|N|133|48|6.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sakakiyama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Diameter 35m |burial = Split-bamboo wooden coffin |burial goods = {{ill|Divine beast mirror|ja|神獣鏡}}, horse-shaped belt hook, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bell, whetstone |Mound type = |Era = Early 5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |other_designation = National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6") |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Okayama |Icon = 円墳 |map_dot_label = Sakakiyama Kofun |map_dot_label position = top }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in type and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6").
== Overview ==
[[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated.
The mound appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s circular, measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|kōyamaki]]. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection.
The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}.
The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster.
== History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National Historic Site.

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Sakakiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukuriyama 1st Sakakiyama Kofun.JPG|280px]] |Caption = Kofun |alternate_name = Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster |location = Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|18.87|N|133|48|6.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sakakiyama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Diameter 35m |burial = Split-bamboo wooden coffin |burial goods = {{ill|Divine beast mirror|ja|神獣鏡}}, horse-shaped belt hook, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bell, whetstone |Mound type = |Era = Early 5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |other_designation = National {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6") |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Okayama |type = 円墳 |map_dot_label = Sakakiyama Kofun |map_dot_label position = top }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in type and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6").
== Overview ==
[[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated.
The mound appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s circular, measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|kōyamaki]]. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection.
The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}.
The tumulus was designated a National other_designation in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster.
== History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National other_designation.
== Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama Kofun Visitor Center (Okayama City, Kita Ward, Shinjo-shimo)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in article composition:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takeo Otani|year=1989|chapter=Sakakiyama Kofun|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Tokyo-do Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Sakakiyama Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Wikicite|reference=Takashi Tsuchiya {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.okayama.jp/kurashi/cmsfiles/contents/0000005/5424/000354583.pdf "The Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster and Northeast Asia -Focusing on Artifacts from Sakakiyama Kofun-"]}}|ref={{Harvid|Takashi Tsuchiya}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Kazuhiro Nishida|year=2020|chapter=|title=The Supergiant Kofun of Kibi: The Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|series=Learn from Archaeological Sites Series, Vol. 148|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kazuhiro Nishida|2020}}}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Sakakiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukuriyama 1st Sakakiyama Kofun.JPG|280px]] |Caption = Kofun |alternate_name = Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster |location = Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|18.87|N|133|48|6.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sakakiyama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Diameter 35m |burial = Split-bamboo wooden coffin |burial goods = {{ill|Divine beast mirror|ja|神獣鏡}}, horse-shaped belt hook, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bell, whetstone |Mound type = |Era = Early 5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |other_designation = National {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6") |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Okayama |type = 円墳 |map_dot_label = Sakakiyama Kofun |map_dot_label position = top }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in type and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designated as a National {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6").
== Overview ==
[[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated.
The mound appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s circular, measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|kōyamaki]]. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. Artifacts attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some artifacts, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection.
The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}.
The tumulus was designated a National other_designation in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster.
== History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National other_designation.

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Sakakiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukuriyama 1st Sakakiyama Kofun.JPG|280px]] |Caption = Kofun |alternate_name = Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster |location = Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|18.87|N|133|48|6.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sakakiyama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Diameter 35m |burial = Split-bamboo wooden coffin |burial goods = {{ill|Divine beast mirror|ja|神獣鏡}}, horse-shaped belt hook, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bell, whetstone |Mound type = |Era = Early 5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |other_designation = National {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6") |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Okayama |type = 円墳 |map_dot_label = Sakakiyama Kofun |map_dot_label position = top }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in type and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is designation1 as a National {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6").
== Overview ==
[[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and, along with five other mounds, forms a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been formally excavated.
The mound appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s circular, measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|kōyamaki]]. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. burial goods attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some burial goods, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection.
The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}.
The tumulus was designation1 a National other_designation in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster.
== History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National other_designation.
== Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama Kofun Visitor Center (Okayama City, Kita Ward, Shinjo-shimo)

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Sakakiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukuriyama 1st Sakakiyama Kofun.JPG|280px]] |Caption = Kofun |alternate_name = Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster |location = Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|18.87|N|133|48|6.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sakakiyama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Diameter 35m |burial = Split-bamboo wooden coffin |burial goods = {{ill|Divine beast mirror|ja|神獣鏡}}, horse-shaped belt hook, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bell, whetstone |Mound type = |Era = Early 5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |other_other_designation = National {{ill|other_other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_other_designation|lt=other_other_designation of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6") |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Okayama |type = 円墳 |map_dot_map_dot_label = Sakakiyama Kofun |map_dot_map_dot_label position = top }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in type and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is other_designation1 as a National {{ill|other_other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_other_designation|lt=other_other_designation of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6").
== Overview ==
[[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and, along with five other mounds, types a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been typeally excavated.
The mound appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s circular, measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|kōyamaki]]. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. burial goods attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some burial goods, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection.
The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}.
The tumulus was other_designation1 a National other_other_designation in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster.
== History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National other_other_designation.
== Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama Kofun Visitor Center (Okayama City, Kita Ward, Shinjo-shimo)
== References == {{Reflist}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Sakakiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukuriyama 1st Sakakiyama Kofun.JPG|280px]] |Caption = Kofun |alternate_name = Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster |location = Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|18.87|N|133|48|6.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sakakiyama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Diameter 35m |burial = Split-bamboo wooden coffin |burial goods = {{ill|Divine beast mirror|ja|神獣鏡}}, horse-shaped belt hook, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bell, whetstone |Mound type = |Era = Early 5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |other_other_designation = National {{ill|other_other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_other_designation|lt=other_other_designation of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6") |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Okayama |type = 円墳 |map_dot_map_dot_label = Sakakiyama Kofun |map_dot_map_dot_label position = top }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in type and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is other_designation1 as a National {{ill|other_other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_other_designation|lt=other_other_designation of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6").
== Overview ==
[[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and, along with five other mounds, types a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been typeally excavated.
The mound appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s circular, measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|kōyamaki]]. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. burial goods attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some burial goods, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection.
The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}.
The tumulus was other_designation1 a National other_other_designation in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster.
== History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National other_other_designation.

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Sakakiyama Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukuriyama 1st Sakakiyama Kofun.JPG|280px]] |Caption = Kofun |alternate_name = Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster |location = Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34|40|18.87|N|133|48|6.87|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sakakiyama Kofun}} |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |area = Diameter 35m |burial = Split-bamboo wooden coffin |burial goods = {{ill|Divine beast mirror|ja|神獣鏡}}, horse-shaped belt hook, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bell, whetstone |Mound type = |Era = Early 5th century |Buried individual = |Imperial mausoleum = |other_designation = National {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6") |Tangible cultural property = |notes = |map = Japan Okayama |type = 円墳 |map_dot_label = Sakakiyama Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Sakakiyama Kofun''' (榊山古墳, Tsukuriyama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Shinjo-shimo, Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. The tumulus is round in type and is part of the {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun Cluster|ja|造山古墳群}}. It is other_designation1 as a National {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} ("Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6").
== Overview ==
[[File:出土地不明 馬形帯鉤.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reference: Unprovenanced horse-shaped belt hook}} {{small|Similar to the horse-shaped belt hook attributed to Sakakiyama Kofun. Okayama Prefectural Museum Collection, photographed during an exhibition at Okayama City Museum.}}]] Sakakiyama Kofun was constructed in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture, on a hill west of the {{ill|Ashimorigawa River|ja|足守川}}. It is located in front of the front projection of {{ill|Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|ja|造山古墳|1=Tsukuriyama Kofun (Okayama)|lt=Tsukuriyama Kofun}} and, along with five other mounds, types a cluster of satellite tombs. Among these tombs, it is the closest to Tsukuriyama Kofun and situated at the highest elevation. Though the mound was looted in 1912 (Meiji 45), it has not been typeally excavated.
The mound appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s circular, measuring [[Category:pp error]]approximately 35 meters in diameter. {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and earthenware fragments have been found on the surface. The burial facility features a {{ill|split-bamboo coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan}} made of [[:en:Sciadopitys verticillata|kōyamaki]]. Due to looting, the mound’s central peak remains collapsed. burial goods attributed to the wooden coffin include six horse-shaped belt hooks, divine beast mirrors, dragon-pattern openwork fittings, perforated bells, and whetstones. Some burial goods, such as the belt hooks potentially crafted in the southern Korean Peninsula and other items originating from northern China, indicate an international connection.
The tomb dates to the early 5th century during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. It is believed to have been constructed after Tsukuriyama Kofun and along with the other satellite tombs. While Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) points to connections with Kyushu, Sakakiyama Kofun reflects ties to the Korean Peninsula, suggesting that the regional ruler buried in Tsukuriyama Kofun had an independent network beyond the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}}.
The tumulus was other_designation1 a National other_designation in 1921 (Taishō 10) as part of the "Tsukuriyama Kofun No. 1 to No. 6" cluster.
== History == * 1912 (Meiji 45): Looting of Senzoku Kofun (No. 5) and Sakakiyama Kofun. * 1913 (Taishō 2): Survey conducted by Chikichi Wada, reported in 1919. * 1921 (Taishō 10): Designated a National other_designation.
== Related Facilities == * Okayama City Tsukuriyama Kofun Visitor Center (Okayama City, Kita Ward, Shinjo-shimo)
